title: Why Low MOQ Changes the Math for Resort Wear Brands
excerpt: A 50 pcs per style per color MOQ lets brands test prints, categories, and price points before committing to deep inventory.
author: Aloha and Co

sections.title: Smarter Print Testing
paragraphs: Resort wear is print-led. A brand may love five artworks on screen, but the market decides which print deserves a larger production run. Starting at 50 pcs per style per color gives teams a practical way to test real demand without locking cash into one oversized order.

sections.title: The 250 Unit Test
paragraphs: Many brands use a 250 unit test: five prints at 50 pcs each. This gives enough inventory for content, wholesale conversations, pop-ups, and early ecommerce testing while avoiding the 1,500+ unit commitment many generalist factories require.

sections.title: Scale After Proof
paragraphs: Once a print sells through, the next production decision becomes clearer. Reorder the winner, retire the weak performer, and use the data to brief the next collection.
